findings.evidence: `rg` shows the matching existing homes at `JHolomorphicEnergy.lean:219` (`fderiv_stdComplexLineEnergyDensity_nonneg`) and `JHolomorphicEnergy.lean:269` (`fderivWithin_stdComplexLineEnergyDensity_nonneg`), while the new counterparts are split into `JHolomorphicEnergyNondegenerate.lean:50` and `:92`.
findings.fix: Move the six derivative nondegeneracy lemmas into `TauCeti/Geometry/Symplectic/JHolomorphicEnergy.lean` next to `fderiv_stdComplexLineEnergyDensity_nonneg` and `fderivWithin_stdComplexLineEnergyDensity_nonneg`, then remove the one-off file unless it still has distinct later material.
findings.issue: The new `fderiv*_stdComplexLineEnergyDensity_{eq_zero_iff,pos_iff,pos}` lemmas are placed in a separate later file even though they are immediate variants of the existing derivative energy API in `JHolomorphicEnergy.lean`.

summary: The imports look justified, but the new nondegeneracy file is the wrong home for the derivative API it contains. These lemmas directly extend the existing standard-line energy API and should live with the matching nonnegativity lemmas in `JHolomorphicEnergy.lean`.
